rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component type scopes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Implementation-level signal routing should be expressed in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections are defined.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architectural structure when inserting AGREE annexes.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
Ensure AGREE contracts are scoped to valid AADL component types or implementations.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes unless explicitly allowed.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
